observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the MA to prepare adjusting entries to record depreciation of depreciable assets with the following suggested entries; and


Provide depreciation expenses on all PPEs when it is available for use such as when it is in the location and condition necessary for it to be capable of operating in the manner intended by management.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the:

Municipal Engineer (ME) to submit all the documents of the completed projects such as the Report on Completed Projects, Certificate of Project Completion and IAR to the MA to support the transfer of CIP to appropriate asset accounts.

The MA to prepare adjusting entries to recognize completed projects to their appropriate asset account; and


The MA to prepare and maintain SL per project recorded under CIP which contains the details and breakdowns of the controlling accounts.

The MA and the ME to coordinate so that projects recorded in the CIP are transferred to the proper asset account after completion.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the MA to:

Moving forward, recognize the Unspent Current Year DRRMF as an expense at year-end by debiting Transfers of Unspent CY DRRMF to TF and crediting Cash in Bank in the GF books;

Recognize the transfer in the TF books by debiting Cash in Bank and crediting TL-DRRM; and

Exert all efforts to determine and submit the details of the TL-DRRMF account in the GF to the COA.


Moreover, we recommended, and Management agreed to revert to the GF the unexpended and unobligated balances of the Capital Outlay amounting to ₱401,300.00 which shall be made continuing in the GF books.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the MA to:

Recognize the discounts for prompt and advance payments of RPT and SET and record penalties on the Fines and Penalties - Property Taxes account, if any, in the period to which they accrue;

Reconcile the RPT and SET Receivables with the record of the Treasury Office and the accounts RPT, RPT Receivables, Deferred RPT Income and SET, SET Receivables and Deferred SET Income, and adjust, if warranted; and

Adhere to the MNGAS on the proper recording of collections and receivables from RPT and SET and recognition of income in the respective books of the GF and SEF.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the MA to make necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct SET Receivables and Deferred SET Income, by debiting Deferred SET Income and crediting SET Receivables by ₱216,592.11.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to

Undertake all efforts to complete the preparation of Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR);

In coordination with the MA and Assessor’s Office, to undertake reconciliation of the balances of the RPTAR and per certified list of RPT with the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts from 2023 and prior years; and

Submit to the Municipal Accountant the Certified List of Taxpayers with Tax Delinquency as of December 31, 2023, including the prior years, after the reconciliation.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the MA to:

Exert all efforts in investigating the cause of the abnormal negative balances in the SL of the Due to LGUs, and thereafter, record the necessary adjustment in the books to fairly present the accounts in the FS; and

Effect the following adjusting entries to reflect the proper balances of the Due to Other Funds account.

Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to direct the coordination of MPDC, MBO and the ME to:
	
Support with SB Resolution the contracts that will be entered into for which payments thereof chargeable against the 20 per cent EDF appropriation which were described in generic terms and in lumpsum appropriation, as enumerated above;

Ensure that the prepared and approved local development and annual investment program submitted by the development council to the local finance committee contains specific proposed projects for inclusion in the annual budget, otherwise, ensure that contracts and disbursements to be charged in the appropriation describe in generic terms in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget be supported with SB Resolution authorizing the charging thereof; and

Initiate the preparation of Program of Works to support the estimate of projects funded under the 20 per cent Economic Development Plan before budget preparation.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to include in the Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender audit. Further, we recommend that the GFPS conduct gender audit on its agency thru the use of GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool.

Management to strictly adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 92-386 on the disposal and derecognition of unserviceable PPEs and direct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to determine the mode of disposal of the unserviceable PPEs.

The Local Chief Executive institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the following activities:
	
To formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical processes and functions and its related steps to be followed to eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the determined disruptions following the prescribed template and be guided by the PSCP guided book; and

To submit plan to the Municipal DRRM Officer for review, to the LCE for approval, and to the SB for the passage of resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.

We also recommended that Management to improve their monitoring of operational requirements like the submission of PSCP.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the MA:
Transfer the completed projects totaling ₱4.757 million and adjust the CIP account thru the following journal entries:
Adjust the erroneous entry noted of one ongoing project thru the following journal entries:
Trace the related transactions in the books of accounts to confirm the completion of these projects, and prepare adjusting entries if needed to properly reclassify transfer to the appropriate asset accounts; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify and reconcile the project data amounting to ₱3.315 million.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The IC conduct a thorough validation and physical inventory of the PPE items under the SEF totaling ₱0.700 million to confirm existence, condition, and actual location of the assets; and
The OMA classify the SEP amounting to ₱264,375.00 to the appropriate semi-expendable inventory accounts, as follows:

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The MHRMO-Designate to:
Prepare a Master List of earned leave credits of the officials and employees of the LGU as of December 31, 2024, and submit to the Local Finance Committee (LFC) thru the Office of the Municipal Budget for consideration in the appropriation for the succeeding years until the earned leave credits are backed up by appropriation and funds;
Prepare the quarterly leave credits earned by officials and employees required under COA Circular 2024-009, with peso equivalent and supported with a copy of its respective leave card, as of every end of the quarter, starting CY 2025; and
Submit the Master List and the quarter report of earned leave credits of officials and employees to the MA for recording in the books subject to the availability of appropriation and funds certified by the MBO and Treasurer.
The MA, upon receipt of the Master List and the quarter report of earned leave credits of officials and employees from the MHRMO, record in the books the liability duly supported by a certification from the MBO and Treasurer on the availability of appropriation and funds.

We recommended, and the OMA agreed to strictly adhere to the appropriate classification of withheld taxes by recording them under the Due to BIR account instead of Accounts Payable to ensure faithful representation of liabilities in the FS.

We recommended, and the OMA agreed to:
Make the necessary adjusting entry to properly recognize the collections to its appropriate income accounts of ₱0.691 million as follows;
Continuously investigate the cause of the abnormal negative balances in the SL of the Due to LGUs, and thereafter, record the necessary adjustment in the books to fairly present the accounts in the FS; and
Effect the following adjusting entries to reflect the proper balances of the Due to Other Funds account in the SEF books:

We recommended, and the OMA agreed to:
Record the Unspent Current Year LDRRMF as an expense at year-end by debiting 5-02-15-010 account instead of 5-02-15-020 account; and
Reconcile the net variance of ₱21,099.94 noted in the Trust Liability-LDRRMF account to reflect accurate balance of the same account as of yearend.
We also reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed to:
Continuously determine and submit the details of the Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF account in the GF to the COA amounting to ₱0.639 million; and
Revert to the GF the unexpended and unobligated balances of the Capital Outlay amounting to ₱401,300.00 which shall be made continuing in the GF books.

Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed that:
The MA to:
Revisit COA Circular No. 2015-008 and provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to FS the LGU’s RN System according to the format prescribed in Annex B of the said circular; and
Revisit COA Circular No. 2012-002 and provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to FS the LGU’s Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF account according to the format prescribed in Annex D of the said circular.
The Inventory Committee to prepare RPCLRN for RN account, rather than RPCPPE, in accordance with the prescribed Annex C format of the same circular, specifically the details of sub-components of local RN.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The OMA maintain and update PPELCs for all PPE items and ensure reconciliation with property records; and
The Office of the General Services Office to maintain and update PCs and ensure reconciliation with accounting records.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the MA to (a) Record the transfer of the cost of the completed project booked as PPE under the TF to the GF amounting to ₱11.481 million; and

(b) Provide the corresponding depreciation expenses, accumulated depreciation and prior period adjustment amounting to ₱0.574 million, ₱1.626 million and ₱9.855 million, respectively, in the GF books in accordance with IPSAS 17.

We recommended, and Management agreed to instruct the MA to:

Effect the immediate cancellation and adjustment of the stale checks in order to reflect the correct balance of the assets account and the fair presentation of the cash in bank account in the FS at year end.

Reflect the reconciling items in the books as adjustments to the affected accounts and moving forward, to properly effect adjustments of reconciling items found in the Bank Reconciliation Statement.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the members of the BAC to properly verify, validate and ascertain the veracity, authenticity and validity of the single largest completed contract of the bidders and other eligibility documents in accordance with the requirements and conditions specified in the bidding documents before declaring and awarding the contract to the winning bidder.

We recommended, and Management agreed to strictly comply with Section 18 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 that Purchase Requests, Request for Quotations and other bidding documents shall not specify the brand names of goods to be procured rather the specification for the procurement of goods shall be based on relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements.

We recommended, and Management agreed to furnish COA advance copies of contracts or Pos issued or entered into by the Municipality together with its supporting documents, consistent with COA Circular 2009-001.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Record the transfer of the cost of the completed project booked as PPE under the TF to the GF amounting to P11.481 million; and
Provide the corresponding depreciation expenses, accumulated depreciation and prior period adjustment amounting to P0.574 million, P1.626 million and P9.855 million, respectively, in the GF books in accordance with IPSAS 17.

14.4 We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to include in the Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender audit. Further, we recommend that the GFPS conduct gender audit on its agency thru the use of GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool.

1.7 We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Inventory Committee to exert their best efforts in completing the required physical inventory taking of properties owned by the LGU and update the RPCPPE with the data of those properties not included in the previous inventory report.
1.8 Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant (MA) to prepare adjusting entries to reconcile the PPE items recorded in the GF books with that of the RPCPPE prepared by the inventory committee.

2.10 We recommended the management to direct the MA to prepare the necessary adjustments and submit the Journal Entry Vouchers and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
2.11 Moreover, revert to the proper cash and liability accounts the amount corresponding to the stale checks to reflect the correct account balances in the financial statements.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office and MPDC commence and fast-tract the implementation of these projects.
3.9 We recommended further the LGU to enact a policy for the implementation of the Livelihood Development Program.
4.4 We recommended that GFPS conduct continuous monitoring on the implementation of GAD PPAs identified in the GAD Plan and Budget.

5.4 We recommended that management to fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions to assist the DRRM Officer in the administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning to assure implementation of plan of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

6.14 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:
The Inventory Committee to assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.
The Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.
The Property Unit to prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.
The LCE to ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.
8.4 We recommended that management provide personal protective equipment and remind all personnel to fully equipped themselves when directly dealing with solid waste as required under RA No. 9003.

9.9 We recommended that management fast-tract the purchase of equipment capable of handling non-biodegradable waste materials to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety.
9.10 Moreover, fast-tract the construction of the building wherein the equipment will be housed to put it into full operation.

10.7 We recommended that the Municipality submit the contracts and purchase orders to the Auditor after its perfection, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office fast-tract the plans and the formation of Program of Works (POW) and commence the implementation of these projects. Provide for additional funds to cover the insufficient project cost allocated for the construction of Toilet in the Municipal Bus Terminal.

We recommended that management stop the practice of charging recurring and/or regular administrative expenses against the 20 per cent development fund. Return the appropriation to the 20 per cent DF which were used to pay for the above mentioned improper charging to align with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 and the recently issued DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01.

We recommended that the LSB to convene at least once a month or as often as the chairman or majority of the members decide to properly and timely monitor the implementation of the programs, projects and activities funded by the SEF.

The Management to require the Municipal Treasurer to record all transactions daily in the Cashbook to monitor the cash in bank balance as of specific date; immediately record the reconciling items in the Cashbook to facilitate proper reconciling in the books; all totals shall be written legibly in ink and the balances shall be carried forward as opening balances of the Cashbook for the next day.

The Management to require the Municipal Accountant to segregate the component of the Local Road Networks namely, (a) road lot, (b) road pavement, (c) drainage and slope protection structures, and (d) other miscellaneous structures, as the case may be, guided by the cited provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008, to accurately reflect the annual accumulated depreciation and the Net Book Value of the account.

The management to file request for write-off the mentioned dormant accounts to ensure for the financial statements are presented fairly. Also direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular verification, analysis, and validation of receivables, un-liquidated cash advances and fund transfers, and determine the concerned debtors, accountable officers and the source and implementing government entities concerned.

The management to:

a. Require the concerned office to follow-up the status regarding the titling of the lot to the DENR for use in the construction of the projects given by the Department of Agriculture as provided in Act. No. 2874 “The Public Land Act”.

b. Require the Municipal Engineer to perform adequate monitoring of the small irrigation project to determine whether the project is undertaken in accordance with the plans and specifications and find out about problems during construction based on actual conditions which may hinder the timely completion of the projects. Gaps/deficiencies noted during monitoring should be communicated immediately to the contractor for correction/appropriate action.

c. Impose liquidated damages pursuant to Section 8, Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 for the delay of the project implementation, as warranted.

d. For further undertakings, require the Municipal Engineering Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office to undertake proper assessment and evaluation, detailed engineering investigations and surveys for the project pursuant to Section 17.6 of the IRR, RA 9184 and consider all possible conditions in the preparation of plans and POW whenever there are funds given from NGAs for development projects.

The Management to:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF-pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

b. Ensure that the unexpended/unobligated balances for MOOE and Quick Response Fund of LDRRMF are transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end in conformity with Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002.

The Management to:

a. Conduct intensive Information, Education and Communication (IEC) campaign on proper segregation of wastes at source through forums, focus group discussions and trainings/workshops to apprise the people on strict implementation of the mandatory segregation of Solid Wastes policy;

b. Allocate a budget for the construction of a fence around the area of the Materials Recovery Facility to avoid dumping of un-segregated wastes from unknown owners; and

      Management Comment:

            The Municipal Treasurer said  that  recently  the  bond  of  the

        least 95% of its monthly  billing.  A  daily  or  weekly  basis  of
        collection might  be  considered  or  introduce  an  early  payment
        discount;


     3. Maintain a Special Account wherein income from water system will be
        kept intact and adopt a policy that the income therein will be used
        for its operation; and lastly


     4. Include in the report the consumption of different offices in order
        to measure correctly the result of operation of the water system.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for all depreciable assets.  Likewise, transfer the property, plant and equipment recorded in Trust Fund to General Fund amounting to P1,172,078.00 in order that corresponding depreciation is recognized.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipality should immediately stop the remittance of more than P100.00 per employee of the government share for HDMF contribution of its regular employees notwithstanding that the personal share of the HDMF contribution is more than the requirement.  Employees who may opt to reduce their contribution to only P100.00 per month may do so by giving a written authority to reduce their monthly contribution.

Henceforth, we will disallow any excess of the government share.  Any budgetary provision in excess of the requirement should be reprogrammed accordingly.
observations.managementAction:
Management commented that the LGU of Kalawit will adhere to the provisions of Section 1 Rule VI of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9679 otherwise known as the Home Development and Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

They gave their assurance that they will follow what is stated in the above provisions and stop immediately the remittance of more than P100.00 per employee of the government share for HDMF contribution of its regular employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management shall see to it that deductions from payroll shall not result to a net take home pay of less than the amount mandated under the provision of the General Appropriations Act for a given year.

Likewise, management shall adopt a strategy that would immediately detect the net pay of its personnel whenever loan application is made, and not to approve the application if the result thereof would reduce the net pay below the statutory requirement.
observations.managementAction: We humbly admit that we have not followed Section 52 of the General Appropriations Act of 2016, dated December 29, 2015. For this year onwards, we commit to abide on the said recommendation by not approving the loan applications if the net take home pay is lower than Three Thousand Five Hundred Pesos (3,500.00).

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that all supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers to avoid audit suspensions and or disallowance.
observations.managementAction:
It has been referred to the OIC-Municipal Accountant who said that they have overlooked this matter since they were only just following the Budget Office in charging accounts.

The Municipal Accountant said that the vouchers have been pre-audited in their office and was surprised on this. Certificates of appearance and others might have been unintentionally detached from vouchers since they will not forward those to the Treasurer’s Office if supporting documents are missing.

In the end, the municipal accountant has committed to follow fundamental principles set forth by law. We will also inform those concerned employees to provide again the missing attachments.

observations.recommendation: The Executive  and Legislative arm of the Municipality is encouraged to make priority in allocating its budget by providing enough funds for the realization of an effective Material Recovery Facility (MRF) as its contribution of achieving the goals as envisioned under RA 9003 and as adopted under its Resolution No. 65 series of 2015 which adopted the Municipal Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan of CY 2015-2025.
observations.managementAction: It has been complied with in the previous years. In fact, we have consistently received the Seal of Good Housekeeping and Seal of Good Local Governance in which it is one of the criteria. But to date, it is our sincerest regret that we unknowingly drifted ways considering that our garbage is not a voluminous as the other cities and municipalities. Aside from that, the usage of our Material Recovery Facility has been stopped since it is located near our water source.
